mission.title: kubevela: [Feature] add dapr addon with pub/sub trait
mission.description: [Feature] add dapr addon with pub/sub trait . Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current kubevela deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevela version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
helm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevela installation.

steps.title: Review kubevela configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela
```
Per offline discussed with  , let's ship dapr trait as a built-in capability. This will make kubevela the best platform to ship dapr apps.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] add dapr addon with pub/sub trait
steps.description:
and I have discussed the next steps for shipping Dapr applications with ease on KubeVela. Here is an update of our plan on KubeVela project:

- Currently to ship a Dapr application on k8s involves [multiple manual steps](https://github.com/dapr/quickstarts/tree/v1.0.0/hello-kubernetes). For
```yaml
kind: Application
    components:
    - name: nodejs-app
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: dapriosamples/hello-k8s-node:latest
      traits:
      - type: dapr
        properties:
          resourceType: state.redis
          provider: crossplane

    - name: python-app
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: dapriosamples/hello-k8s-python:latest
      traits:
      - type: dapr
        properties:
          resourceType: state.redis
          provider: crossplane
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] add dapr addon with pub/sub trait " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
and I have discussed the next steps for shipping Dapr applications with ease on KubeVela. Here is an update of our plan on KubeVela project:

- Currently to ship a Dapr application on k8s involves [multiple manual steps](https://github.com/dapr/quickstarts/tree/v1.0.0/hello-kubernetes).
codeSnippets:
kind: Application
    components:
    - name: nodejs-app
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: dapriosamples/hello-k8s-node:latest
      traits:
      - type: dapr
        properties:
          resourceType: state.redis
          provider: crossplane

    - name: python-app
      type: webservice
      properties:
        image: dapriosamples/hello-k8s-python:latest
      traits:
      - type: dapr
        properties:
          resourceType: state.redis
          provider: crossplane

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevela installed or the issue environment reproducible.
